business.headline: IT, PSU bank stocks lead gains as markets extend rally on falling oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 347.14 points, or 0.45%, to settle at 77,155.62, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 96.55 points, or 0.40%, to close at 24,085.70.

IDBI Bank surges 19%: What's driving the sudden buying frenzy?IDBI Bank shares surged nearly 19% as renewed privatisation hopes and heavy trading lifted the stock. The rally reflects investor focus on the stalled stake sale, block deals and improving fundamentals.

SpaceX enters world's top 5 club: Elon Musk's company now worth more than AmazonSpaceX jumped past Amazon within days of its market debut after a sharp Wall Street rally. The surge, driven partly by options trading and index inclusion hopes, has intensified debate over its valuation.

Air India launches 'Basic' fare on domestic flights. What's included, what's notAir India has introduced a new Basic fare on select domestic routes that excludes complimentary meals. The pilot gives budget-conscious travellers a lower entry fare while leaving existing full-service fare options intact.

KPMG's AI-written report praising AI withdrawn after fake cases surfaceKPMG withdrew its global AI report after several organisations challenged fabricated case studies. The episode has sharpened concerns over AI hallucinations and the need for human verification.

A trade every 35 minutes? Trump's portfolio activity raises eyebrowsA financial disclosure showed US President Donald Trump's investment accounts made 3,642 trades in 90 days. The filing has prompted ethics questions, calls for an investigation and debate over conflicts of interest.

India isn't facing a 1991 crisis. But another challenge is taking shapeIndia has built a formidable forex cushion. But in an increasingly unpredictable world, will that be enough if foreign money starts drying up?

Stock market opens higher for fourth day as Brent crude slips below $79The S&P BSE Sensex was up 179.03 points, or 0.23%, at 76,987.51 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 46.45 points, or 0.19%, to trade at 24,035.60. The Nifty once again remained above the psychologically important 24,000 mark.

Jio may file $4 billion IPO papers before Reliance AGMReliance Jio may file draft papers for a $4 billion IPO within days, possibly before Reliance Industries' Friday AGM. The move would revive focus on one of India's most anticipated listings after delays and a slowdown in the IPO market.

Pizza Hut sold for $2.7 billion: Pizza chain many grew up with gets a new ownerYum Brands has agreed to sell Pizza Hut for $2.7 billion, splitting China from the rest of the business. The deal gives Yum China greater control in a key market and offers LongRange Capital a chance to revive the global chain.

Gold rallies, oil prices retreat on optimism over US-Iran peace dealGold rose on Tuesday after an interim US-Iran deal eased pressure on oil prices. Lower inflation fears also reduced expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike later this year.

Why India's wealthy are quietly buying second residencies abroadThey are not leaving India. But they want the option to, just in case. From Dubai to Portugal to Greece, promoters and business families are spending crores on second residencies abroad. It is not about emigration. It is about insurance.

8th Pay Commission: Memorandum deadline ends. What have unions asked forThe deadline for 8th Pay Commission proposals has ended, bringing employee and pensioner demands before the panel. The next phase will test which calls on salary, DA, fitment and pensions shape the final recommendations.

Sensex ends 544 points higher, Nifty nears 24,000; HUL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 544.15 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 76,808.48, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 135.25 points, or 0.57%, to close at 23,989.15, just shy of the crucial 24,000 mark.

11 digital tricks your bank can no longer play on youThe RBI has named and banned 11 specific digital manipulation tactics that banks use on their apps and websites to push products you never asked for. Here is what each one means and how it was being used against you.

US-Iran deal eases oil, but India's petrol relief may take longerThe US-Iran deal has reopened the Strait of Hormuz and cooled crude prices. But India may not see immediate fuel relief as supply chains and inventories recover.

World's 1st trillionaire says a quadrillion-dollar fortune 'not impossible'Elon Musk said becoming the world's first quadrillionaire is "not impossible" and linked it to factories on the Moon and Mars. He also said dollars may no longer matter in a future shaped by AI, robots and interplanetary industry.

Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high as West Asia war fuels inflation fearsThe Bank of Japan raised its policy rate to 1% from 0.75%, the highest level since 1995, citing faster pass-through from crude oil prices. The move signalled concern that oil-led cost pressures could push inflation above target while keeping focus on gradual further hikes.

Dalal Street extends gains as Iran-US peace deal eases inflation concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 306.22 points, or 0.40%, to 76,570.55 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 72.95 points, or 0.31%, to 23,926.85 as of 9:36 am.

Finally, RBI takes your side, comes hard on banks for mis-sellingThe RBI has issued binding directions that ban forced bundling, mis-selling and manipulative digital sales tactics by commercial banks. From January 1, 2027, the rules will introduce mandatory refunds, tighter consent norms and direct accountability for agents.

entertainment.headline: Sanchita Ugale death: Smita Bansal, Sarwam Kulkarni call for mental health support Television actor Sanchita Ugale's death has prompted emotional reactions from the industry. Read on to find out who left behind secure professions to become some of the biggest names on streaming platforms.

Samay Raina's Latent 2 gets a 'heavily guarded' Netflix tease, fans bring the spice Netflix posted a cryptic teaser featuring Samay Raina's bodyguard and tagged the comedian. The post has stirred speculation around India's Got Latent 2, though no formal announcement has followed.

Why was actor Arya's cameo removed from Suriya's Karuppu? He answers Actor Arya has opened up about the reason his scenes were removed from Suriya's latest film Karuppu. The actor revealed that director RJ Balaji personally informed him about the decision to chop his scenes.

Can CM Vijay save Jana Nayagan amid censor row? Distributor refund reports emerge Vijay's Jana Nayagan remains stalled with the censor board, with the team making another push in New Delhi. The prolonged delay has strained distributors and prompted reports that Vijay may step in financially.

Dark future, depression: What actor Sanchita Ugale's Insta chat says about her pain Actor Indraxi Kanjilal recalled her final conversations with Sanchita Ugale and alleged on-set harassment. Her account has again drawn attention to the professional and personal struggles actors can face.

How The Mummy's iconic villain Imhotep shaped Ranabaali's villain Arnold Vosloo Hollywood actor Arnold Vosloo will make his Indian cinema debut in the Telugu film Ranabaali, where he plays Sir Theodore Hector. Tom Holland and Zendaya serve peak Peter-MJ energy in new Spider-Man interviews Actors Tom Holland and Zendaya are back promoting Spider-Man: Brand New Day, but it's not just the film that's got fans talking. One comment from Holland has reignited wedding buzz and sent social media into a frenzy.

While Asha Banks and Matthew Broome remain watchable, the sequel mistakes endless drama for emotional depth.

Manoj Bajpayee not the first choice for The Family Man, this Telugu superstar was Filmmaker Raj Nidimoru, in a recent interview, revealed that they had considered a Telugu superstar to play Manoj Bajpayee's role in The Family Man. Also, he explained that the show was initially conceived as a film.

Samantha scores pre-release box office win as Maa Inti Bangaram turns profitable Actor Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaram has recovered its full investment before its June 19 release. The pre-release business was driven by firm demand for rights across territories and platforms.

Ravi Kishan reacts to actor Sanchita Ugale's suicide, advocates meditation Ravi Kishan reacted after actor Sanchita Ugale died by suicide in Mumbai at 26. He emphasised the significance of meditation, spirituality, and emotional strength to navigate life's hardships and urged everyone to keep hope alive.

general news.headline: Will go back to dropping bombs if...: Trump says Iran deal not finalThe strong remarks by Donald Trump at the G7 Summit in France, just two days before the US and Iran are to formally sign the peace deal MoU in Switzerland, indicate the fragility of the agreement.

No hug, just a handshake. Can today's Modi-Trump G7 talks repair ties?Narendra Modi and Donald Trump met at the G7 Summit after 16 months amid visible restraint. Modi's remarks on trust and the lives of Indian seafarers underscored the strain ahead of their bilateral talks today.

Here's whyWest Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari announced the recruitment of more than 1,000 Gorkha youths into the Eastern Frontier Rifles, which Darjeeling MP Raju Bista said, was "Mamata Banerjee was trying to kill". Her allegations stand in stark contrast to India's track record and the growth of minorities in the country.

Aman and Asif to get $2.7 billion each after Elon Musk buys Cursor for $60 billionAman Sanger, a techie with Indian roots, and Pakistan-born Sualeh Asif, just became billionaires. The two are reportedly set to earn as much as $2.7 billion each after Elon Musk's SpaceX acquired Cursor for $60 billion.

The Kota perspective: Trust, rumours and the Telegram debate before NEET re-testHandcrafted ByOur Editors Students in Kota preparing for the June 21 NEET reexamination say they continue to encounter claims of paper leaks through Telegram, WhatsApp and phone calls. While the government has moved to restrict Telegram ahead of the exam, conversations on the ground suggest many students and teachers view the platform as only one part of a much larger ecosystem fuelled by rumours, anxiety and misinformation.

Why this year's monsoon delay is giving Mumbai nightmaresMumbai's delayed monsoon is stretching into late June as reservoir levels stay low. The prolonged wait has sharpened fears over water supplies despite contingency steps and a supply cut.

When India removed barricades from US Embassy and why the 2013 memory is back in 2026In 2013, the arrest and strip-search of an Indian diplomat in New York prompted a fierce and coordinated diplomatic retaliation from New Delhi. Live: Rebel Sena UBT MPs seek merger with Shinde Sena in letter to SpeakerShiv Sena UBT Crisis Live Updates: Amid speculation of a possible shift within Shiv Sena (UBT), sources clarified that no meeting took place between Shrikant Shinde and UBT MPs, contrary to earlier reports. They said any political move would first require the rebel group to submit a formal letter to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. The clarification comes amid renewed chatter over Uddhav Thackeray's parliamentary strength following reports of possible defections, while party leaders continue internal consultations and outreach. Sanjay Raut told India Today that it is difficult to identify camp-wise alignment unless all MPs are together, adding that the real test will be tomorrow's legislative meeting for which a whip has been issued. He further said no one leaves a party voluntarily and that MPs should resign openly if they wish to switch sides. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Austria score 3 past Jordan to go second behind Argentina in Group J FIFA World Cup 2026: Austria beat Jordan 3-1 in Santa Clara after Yazan Al-Arab's own goal shattered a 1-1 deadlock. Jordan's spirited World Cup debut still forced Austria to rely on a stoppage-time penalty to close it out.

Should Lionel Messi have seen red vs Algeria? Fans debate decision FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi hit a hat-trick as Argentina beat Algeria 3-0 in their World Cup opener. A disputed challenge on Aissa Mandi, ignored by the referee and VAR, kept the noise alive.
